skill.name: literatureimages-interpretation
skill.description: Interpret figures in academic papers and their captions when the input is a PDF-to-Markdown document with page markers and image links, producing a structured Markdown report for extracting variables, trends, and conclusions.
skill.summary: Automated figure-by-figure analysis of academic paper images for structured data extraction and reporting.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 43 static findings are false positives. This is a documentation-only skill (Markdown instructions for AI agents), not executable code. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings triggered on Markdown code formatting and inline code examples. 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings matched metadata content hashes and benign string patterns. 'System reconnaissance' findings matched normal validation text. 'Hardcoded URL' is a source repository attribution link. The skill's own audit (literatureimages-interpretation_audit_result_v1.json) confirms security PASS, veto gate PASS, and grade 'Production Ready' (85/100). No executable code, network calls, or malicious patterns were found after human evaluation.

content.user_title: Interpret Figures from Academic Research Papers
content.value_statement: Researchers spend hours manually extracting data from paper figures and charts. This skill automates figure-by-figure interpretation by parsing PDF-to-Markdown documents with image links and producing structured reports with variables, trends, and conclusions.
actual_capabilities: Parses PDF-to-Markdown documents with page markers and image links for figure extraction.
Opens every image in the images folder and classifies each as chart, table, schematic, flowchart, or body-text block.
Aligns images to figure numbers using captions and in-text citations.
Produces evidence-based interpretations with variables, trends, comparisons, and conclusions.
Filters out non-figure content such as scanned body text blocks.
Saves structured Markdown reports to the outputs directory for downstream processing.
limitations: Requires pre-converted PDF-to-Markdown input with page markers and image links.
Cannot interpret figures from native PDF files without prior conversion.
Interpretation quality depends on visible image content and caption quality.
Does not generate image list files or export internal alignment tables.

use_cases.title: Systematic Literature Review Data Extraction
use_cases.description: A researcher conducting a meta-analysis needs to extract statistical data from figures across dozens of papers.

use_cases.title: Medical Paper Figure Analysis
use_cases.description: A medical student must interpret clinical trial figures including survival curves, bar charts, and forest plots for a research project.

use_cases.title: Knowledge Base Ingestion Pipeline
use_cases.description: A data science team ingesting research papers into a knowledge base needs standardized figure interpretations for downstream analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Figure Interpretation Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Read the PDF-to-Markdown document in inputs/ and identify all figures. For each chart, extract the variables shown, main trends, and key conclusions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick figure scan for a single paper

prompt_templates.title: Detailed Comparative Figure Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Open every image in the images folder. Classify each as chart, table, schematic, flowchart, or body-text block. For chart-type images only, produce a detailed interpretation including variables, main trends, comparisons, statistical significance, and conclusions. Use the template from assets/figure_interpretation_template.md.
prompt_templates.scenario: Full paper analysis with template output

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Paper Batch Processing
prompt_templates.prompt: Process all Markdown files in the inputs/ folder. For each file, locate the matching images folder, classify images, align figures using captions, and output one interpretation report per file to outputs/.

prompt_templates.title: Evidence-Based Figure Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Review each figure interpretation and verify that every claim is grounded in visible image content, caption text, or relevant body text. Mark any unsupported claims as Not Specified. Follow quality checks in references/guide.md.

output_examples.input: PDF-to-Markdown document for RateSOX2.md containing Figure 1A survival curve, Figure 2 bar chart, and Figure 3 schematic.
output_examples.output:
## Image Interpretation

### Figure 1A
- Figure Number: Figure 1A
- Figure Type: Kaplan-Meier survival curve
- Variables/Indicators: Time (days) on x-axis, Survival probability on y-axis
- Main Trends: SOX2-high group shows lower survival probability over 60 days
- Key Comparisons: SOX2-high vs SOX2-low groups, p<0.01
- Statistical Significance: Log-rank test p=0.008
- Conclusion: Higher SOX2 expression correlates with poorer prognosis
- Uncertainties: Censoring markers not clearly visible below 20% survival

output_examples.input: Markdown document with Figure 2 showing a bar chart comparing gene expression levels across three treatment conditions.
output_examples.output:
### Figure 2
- Figure Number: Figure 2
- Figure Type: Grouped bar chart with error bars
- Variables/Indicators: Treatment condition (Control, Drug A, Drug B) on x-axis, Relative expression (fold change) on y-axis
- Main Trends: Drug B shows 2.5-fold reduction compared to Control
- Key Comparisons: Drug A (1.2-fold) vs Drug B (2.5-fold) vs Control (baseline)
- Statistical Significance: Error bars represent SD, n=3 independent experiments
- Conclusion: Drug B significantly reduces target gene expression
- Uncertainties: Sample size per group not specified in caption

best_practices: Always classify every image before interpretation to avoid treating body-text blocks as charts.
Base interpretations strictly on visible image content, caption text, and relevant body text citations without speculation.
Save output as UTF-8 Markdown to prevent character corruption in downstream processing.
anti_patterns: Do not skip images or cherry-pick only familiar figure types; examine every image in the folder.
Do not export the internal image alignment list; the final output must contain only the Image Interpretation section.
Do not infer conclusions beyond the evidence shown in figures; mark missing information as Not Specified.

faq.question: What input format does this skill require?
faq.answer: The skill requires a PDF-to-Markdown document containing page markers like ## Page XX and Markdown image links. Native PDF files must be converted first.

faq.question: Can this skill process PDF files directly?
faq.answer: No. PDF files must be converted to Markdown using a tool like pdf-extract before this skill can process them.

faq.question: What types of images does the skill classify?
faq.answer: Images are classified into chart/plot, table, schematic, flowchart, or body-text block. Only chart-type and figure-like visuals are interpreted.

faq.question: How does the skill handle unlabeled figures?
faq.answer: Figures that cannot be matched to a figure number using captions or in-text citations are labeled as Unassigned. They are still interpreted if classified as chart-type.

faq.question: Where are the output files saved?
faq.answer: Output files are saved to the outputs/ folder as one UTF-8 Markdown file per input document. The filename is based on the input literature name.

faq.question: Does the skill create any intermediate files?
faq.answer: No. The internal image alignment list is maintained only for processing and is not exported. The final output contains only the Image Interpretation section.
